items.title: Factbox-Major deals involving U.S. drugmakers and biotechs over the past decade
items.description: Merck is acquiring UK-based Verona Pharma for about $10 billion, as part of the U.S. drugmaker's strategy to diversify beyond its blockbuster cancer treatment Keytruda. The move adds to a growing list of high-profile transactions over the past decade by U.S. pharmaceutical companies to acquire promising therapies in fields ranging from oncology, neurology to rare diseases and obesity. Globally, $105.3 billion worth of pharmaceuticals and biotech M&A deals have been inked so far in 2025, according to data compiled by LSEG, up 7% from year-ago levels and the highest year-to-date total since 2023.

items.title: Wall Street waves off Trump's 200% pharma tariff threat
items.description: President Trump threatened as much as 200% tariffs on pharma Tuesday. By Wednesday morning, Wall Street had ignored the threat.

items.title: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) Submits sNDA For Schizophrenia Drug CAPLYTA® To FDA
items.description: Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) submitted a supplemental New Drug Application for CAPLYTA®, aiming to prevent schizophrenia relapse, marking a significant step in expanding its mental health portfolio. Over the last quarter, Johnson & Johnson's stock price increased by 4%. This movement aligns with the broader market trends, which have shown steady growth despite some trade uncertainties.
